title: Maharajbagh zoo
tagline: Maharajbagh zoo — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: Maharajbagh zoo is the central zoo of Nagpur, India.
overview.description: Maharajbagh zoo is the central zoo of Nagpur, India. The zoo is located in the heart of the city and has been built on the garden of the Bhonsle and Maratha rulers of the city. The zoo comes under the Central Zoo Authority (CZA) of India and is maintained by the Panjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Vidyapeeth (PKV) of Nagpur. The zoo had come under scrutiny in 2006 for possible closure due to issues concerning animal healthcare but was cleared of all objections raised by CZA in 2007.

nearestAirport.name: Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar International Airport, Nagpur

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jhansi Rani Square metro station
topPlaces.description: Jhansi Rani Square is an elevated metro station on the East-West corridor of the Aqua Line of Nagpur Metro in Nagpur, India. It was opened on 28 January 2020.

topPlaces.name: Cotton Market metro station
topPlaces.description: Cotton Market is an elevated metro station on the East-West corridor of the Aqua Line of Nagpur Metro in Nagpur, India. It was opened on 21 September 2023.

topPlaces.name: Yashwant Stadium
topPlaces.description: Yashwant Stadium is a football stadium in Nagpur, India. It has a maximum seating capacity of 30,000 and a maximum standing capacity of 50,000.

topPlaces.name: Sitabuldi metro station
topPlaces.description: Sitabuldi is an elevated metro interchange station on the North-South corridor of the Orange Line and East-West corridor of the Aqua Line of Nagpur Metro in the city of Nagpur, Maharashtra, India which allows passengers to transfer between the Orange Line and the Aqua Line. It was opened to the public on 8 March 2019.

topPlaces.name: Zero Mile Stone (Nagpur)
topPlaces.description: Zero Mile Stone (ISO: Śūnya Mailācā Dagaḍa) is a monument built by the British during the Great Trigonometrical Survey of India in 1907 in Nagpur, Maharashtra. The Zero Mile Stone consists of a pillar made up of sandstone and another small stone representing the GTS Standard Bench Mark, and four stucco horses that were added later.

topPlaces.name: Nagpur Central Museum
topPlaces.description: The Nagpur Central Museum, popularly known as Ajab Bangala, is located in Nagpur, Maharashtra, India. Established in 1863, Nagpur Central Museum is one of the oldest museums in India and Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Institution of Engineers metro station
topPlaces.description: Institution of Engineers, also known as Institute of Engineers, is an elevated metro station on the East-West corridor of the Aqua Line of Nagpur Metro in Nagpur, India. It was opened on 28 January 2020.

topPlaces.name: Meetha Neem Dargah
topPlaces.description: Meetha-Neem Dargah also known as Meetha-Neem Sharif is the resting place of Aala Hazrat Sufi Saint Sayyad Jalaluddin Meer Surkh. It is located at civil lines region of Nagpur.

Cotton Market is an elevated metro station on the East-West corridor of the Aqua Line of Nagpur Metro in Nagpur, India. It was opened on 21 September 2023.
Yashwant Stadium is a football stadium in Nagpur, India. It has a maximum seating capacity of 30,000 and a maximum standing capacity of 50,000.

Sitabuldi is an elevated metro interchange station on the North-South corridor of the Orange Line and East-West corridor of the Aqua Line of Nagpur Metro in the city of Nagpur, Maharashtra, India which allows passengers to transfer between the Orange Line and the Aqua Line. It was opened to the public on 8 March 2019.

Zero Mile Stone (ISO: Śūnya Mailācā Dagaḍa) is a monument built by the British during the Great Trigonometrical Survey of India in 1907 in Nagpur, Maharashtra. The Zero Mile Stone consists of a pillar made up of sandstone and another small stone representing the GTS Standard Bench Mark, and four stucco horses that were added later.
The Nagpur Central Museum, popularly known as Ajab Bangala, is located in Nagpur, Maharashtra, India. Established in 1863, Nagpur Central Museum is one of the oldest museums in India and Maharashtra.

Meetha-Neem Dargah also known as Meetha-Neem Sharif is the resting place of Aala Hazrat Sufi Saint Sayyad Jalaluddin Meer Surkh. It is located at civil lines region of Nagpur.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Maharajbagh zoo?
faq.a: The best time to visit Maharajbagh zoo is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Maharajbagh zoo?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar International Airport, Nagpur (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Nagpur Junction (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Maharajbagh zoo?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Maharajbagh zoo at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Maharajbagh zoo?
faq.a: Stays in Maharajbagh zoo start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Maharajbagh zoo?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jhansi Rani Square metro station, Cotton Market metro station, Yashwant Stadium, Sitabuldi metro station, Zero Mile Stone (Nagpur).
